model_name_policy: Every recommended_models[].model value must not be normalized before passing to --model.
selection_rules: Use the first recommended model unless cost, latency, privacy, or provider availability pushes you to another listed model.
Claude Code models are last-resort fallbacks: dispatch to them only when every non-Claude entry is exhausted — except architecture_design and security_review, where fable is rank 1.
Use High, Thinking, fable, opus, or gpt-5.6-sol for ambiguous, high-risk, multi-file, long-context, security, or architecture work.
Use Low, mini, haiku, gpt-5.6-luna, or GPT-OSS 120B (Medium) for bounded, repetitive, low-risk, or private/offline work.
Use provider diversity for review: ask a different provider than the one that produced the first draft.

codex.gpt-5.6-terra.role: Balanced agentic coding model for everyday work.

codex.gpt-5.6-luna.role: Fast and affordable agentic coding model.

codex.gpt-5.5.role: OpenAI flagship for complex reasoning, coding, tools, and long-context professional work.

codex.gpt-5.4.role: Strong general coding and professional-work model with good cost/performance.

codex.gpt-5.4-mini.role: Fast, lower-cost option for simpler coding, test, doc, and subagent-style tasks.

antigravity.gemini-3.5-flash-medium.role: Default balanced Gemini option for agentic coding, iterative implementation, and most tasks.

antigravity.gemini-3.5-flash-high.role: Higher-reasoning Gemini option for hard coding, planning, tool use, and debugging.

antigravity.gemini-3.5-flash-low.role: Fast Gemini option for simple analysis, writing, and low-risk code loops.

antigravity.gemini-3.1-pro-high.role: Deep-reasoning, multimodal, long-context option for broad datasets and complex tradeoffs.

antigravity.claude-sonnet-4.6-thinking.role: Balanced high-quality Claude option for coding, agent planning, knowledge work, and design.

antigravity.claude-opus-4.6-thinking.role: Careful, long-context Claude option for hard planning, debugging, code review, and large codebases.

antigravity.gpt-oss-120b-medium.role: Open-weight high-reasoning option for private, local, offline, or vendor-diverse work.

claude-code.fable.role: Top Claude Code alias for long-running agentic work, hard planning, and expert review.

claude-code.opus.role: Claude Code alias for complex agentic coding, large refactors, root-cause analysis, and review.

claude-code.sonnet.role: Default Claude Code alias for everyday coding, planning, review, docs, and tests.

claude-code.haiku.role: Fast Claude Code alias for simple, bounded, low-risk writing and coding tasks.

product_owner.description: Maximizes the value of the product by owning the Product Backlog — ordering, clarifying, and communicating work items so the team always builds the most valuable thing next.
accountabilities: Define and communicate product goal and vision
Create, order, and refine Product Backlog items
Ensure the backlog is transparent, visible, and understood
Accept or reject Increments against acceptance criteria
Stakeholder engagement and expectation management

scrum_master.description: Accountable for the Scrum Team's effectiveness. Serves the team by facilitating Scrum events, removing impediments, and coaching the organization on empirical process control.
accountabilities: Facilitate Scrum events (Sprint Planning, Daily Scrum, Sprint Review, Sprint Retrospective)
Coach the team on self-management and cross-functionality
Remove impediments to the team's progress
Ensure all Scrum artifacts maintain transparency
Help the organization adopt Scrum and empirical practices

software_engineer.description: Cross-functional team member committed to creating a usable Increment each Sprint. Responsible for all aspects of implementation — coding, testing, integrating, and verifying working software.
accountabilities: Create a plan for the Sprint (Sprint Backlog)
Adapt the daily plan toward the Sprint Goal
Hold each other accountable as professionals
Write, test, and integrate production-quality code
Participate in code review and pair programming

tech_lead.description: Provides technical leadership and architectural vision across the team or Agile Release Train. Guides system design decisions, ensures technical alignment with business goals, and maintains the architectural runway.
accountabilities: Define and maintain architectural vision and runway
Guide technical decisions and design tradeoffs
Lead code review for architectural consistency
Ensure non-functional requirements (performance, scalability, security) are met
Mentor engineers on design patterns and best practices

qa_engineer.description: Ensures quality throughout the SDLC by designing test strategies, writing and automating tests, validating acceptance criteria, and driving continuous testing practices within the Agile team.
accountabilities: Define test strategy (unit, integration, e2e, acceptance)
Write and maintain automated test suites
Validate acceptance criteria and edge cases
Perform exploratory testing and regression verification
Report defects and verify fixes

ux_ui_designer.description: Owns user experience and interface design. Works iteratively with the team to research user needs, create prototypes, design UX flows, and ensure accessibility and interaction design meet user expectations.
accountabilities: Conduct user research and usability testing
Design UX flows, wireframes, and interactive prototypes
Maintain design system consistency
Ensure accessibility (a11y) and interaction design standards
Collaborate with developers on frontend implementation fidelity

devops_engineer.description: Bridges development and operations by building and maintaining CI/CD pipelines, automating deployments, managing infrastructure as code, and ensuring smooth, repeatable release processes.
accountabilities: Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines
Automate deployment, rollback, and migration processes
Manage infrastructure as code (IaC)
Configure and maintain development environments and workspaces
Support release planning and deployment checklists

security_engineer.description: Embeds security into every phase of the SDLC by conducting threat modeling, implementing automated security scanning (SAST/DAST/SCA), reviewing auth and data boundaries, and driving secure design practices.
accountabilities: Conduct threat modeling and secure design review
Implement and maintain security scanning in CI/CD pipelines
Review auth, data boundary, and access control implementations
Triage vulnerabilities and coordinate remediation
Define security policies and compliance guardrails

sre.description: Ensures system reliability, availability, and performance in production. Manages error budgets, automates operational toil, designs monitoring and alerting, and leads incident response and postmortem processes.
accountabilities: Define and manage SLOs, SLIs, and error budgets
Design monitoring, alerting, and observability systems
Lead incident response, triage, and postmortem processes
Automate operational toil and runbook procedures
Advise on architecture for reliability and scalability

engineering_manager.description: Owns the 'how' and 'who' of engineering execution. Manages team health, hiring, technical quality, and delivery cadence. Partners with Product Manager/Owner to balance scope, quality, and timeline trade-offs.
accountabilities: People management — hiring, coaching, career growth
Technical delivery oversight and quality standards
Sprint planning facilitation and capacity management
Technical debt prioritization and architectural decisions
Cross-team coordination and dependency management

product_manager.description: Defines product vision, strategy, and roadmap at the program or ART level. Owns the ART backlog, prioritizes features using WSJF, and bridges market/customer needs with engineering capacity across multiple teams.
accountabilities: Define product vision, strategy, and roadmap
Own and prioritize the ART backlog (features and enablers)
Conduct market research and competitive analysis
Align stakeholders on product direction and trade-offs
Validate product-market fit and business outcomes

technical_writer.description: Ensures clear, accurate, and maintainable documentation for developers, users, and stakeholders. Works iteratively with the team, participating in sprint planning to align documentation with feature delivery.
accountabilities: Write and maintain developer docs, API references, and ADRs
Create onboarding guides and knowledge base articles
Write release notes, changelogs, and handoff documentation
Review code comments and inline documentation quality
Collaborate with engineers on doc-as-code workflows

business_analyst.description: Bridges business stakeholders and the technical team by analyzing business requirements, refining user stories, mapping business processes, and ensuring the team understands the functional needs of the business.
accountabilities: Elicit and analyze business requirements and constraints
Translate business needs into user stories and acceptance criteria
Map and optimize business processes
Conduct feasibility and impact analysis
Facilitate stakeholder workshops and requirements sessions

data_analyst.description: Explores data sources, identifies data-driven insights, and translates quantitative analysis into actionable requirements or product decisions. Supports discovery with market data and retrospectives with delivery metrics.
accountabilities: Analyze data to inform product and engineering decisions
Build dashboards, reports, and data visualizations
Define and track delivery and product metrics
Support discovery with quantitative market and user research
Provide data for retrospective process analysis

agile_coach.framework_origin: SAFe (RTE at ART level), Scrum Alliance
agile_coach.description: Operates across multiple teams and organizational levels as a strategic change agent. Mentors Scrum Masters, Product Owners, and leadership on Agile mindsets. Drives enterprise-wide process improvement and removes systemic organizational barriers.
accountabilities: Drive organizational Agile transformation and cultural change
Coach Scrum Masters, Product Owners, and leadership
Facilitate cross-team retrospectives and improvement experiments
Identify and remove systemic organizational impediments
Assess and improve team maturity and Agile adoption

stakeholder.description: Provides business context, defines strategic goals, and participates in Sprint Reviews to inspect Increments and give feedback. In SAFe, Business Owners are accountable for the business outcomes of the Agile Release Train.
accountabilities: Provide business vision, strategy, and funding context
Participate in Sprint Reviews and PI System Demos
Give feedback on delivered Increments and product direction
Approve or adjust business priorities and constraints
Champion the product within the organization

discovery_research.agile_context: Product goal discovery, domain research, user/problem exploration, market and technical feasibility.

recommended_models.why: Strong for complex synthesis and professional reasoning.
Good for broad-context, multimodal, and dataset-heavy research.
Careful long-context research and expert-grade synthesis.
Balanced research when latency matters.
Best when the research may turn into long-running agentic planning.

requirements_user_stories.agile_context: Write user stories, acceptance criteria, edge cases, constraints, and definition of done.

recommended_models.why: Strong instruction following, knowledge work, and design detail.
Solid professional writing plus coding awareness.
Fast for straightforward story cleanup.
Best default for clear, practical story/spec writing.
Cheap fast option for low-risk formatting and story splitting.

backlog_refinement_prioritization.agile_context: Split backlog items, refine scope, estimate, find dependencies, rank by value and risk.

recommended_models.why: Good for product, design, and implementation tradeoffs.
Good agentic planner for iterative backlog work.
Good cost/performance for everyday engineering planning.
Fast option for small, obvious refinement.
Default for practical backlog cleanup and task splitting.

sprint_planning.agile_context: Create sprint goals, implementation plans, task breakdowns, dependency checks, and risk notes.

recommended_models.why: Best OpenAI option for complex reasoning and coding plans.
Careful planning for larger codebases and long-horizon tasks.
High-effort Gemini path for hard code and agent plans.
Use when the plan may need long-running agentic execution.
Default for normal sprint/task plans.

architecture_design.agile_context: System design, architecture tradeoffs, data model/API design, migration strategy, non-functional requirements.

recommended_models.why: Strongest option for high-stakes long-horizon architecture.
Strong complex reasoning and coding-aware design.
Careful design review for large codebases and edge cases.
Good for broad multimodal/long-context tradeoff analysis.
Good Claude Code option for implementation-oriented architecture.

ui_ux_prototyping.agile_context: UX flows, frontend prototypes, design-system-aware UI implementation, accessibility and interaction design.

recommended_models.why: Strong balance of frontend code, design sense, and knowledge work.
Strong for frontend coding and computer-use workflows.
Use for complex frontend architecture or production-grade flows.
Good for coding agents and interactive prototyping.
Good default for everyday frontend work.

implementation_coding.agile_context: Implement backlog items, refactor small areas, wire APIs, edit code, and run local verification.

recommended_models.why: Strong coding model with good cost/performance.
Good for iterative coding loops and agentic execution.
Use for higher-quality implementation and design-sensitive code.
Fast for small, bounded changes.
Best default for everyday implementation.

debugging_root_cause.agile_context: Reproduce bugs, trace callers, find root cause, patch shared logic, and verify regressions.

recommended_models.why: Strong at careful debugging, code review, and large codebases.
Strong for hard reasoning and coding fixes.
Good for hard coding/debugging with tools.
Good Claude Code path for root-cause work.
Default for normal bugfixes.

code_review_quality.agile_context: Review diffs, find regressions, check maintainability, identify missing tests, and validate acceptance criteria.

recommended_models.why: Strong code review and mistake-catching on larger codebases.
Good independent frontier review.
Good balanced review for normal PRs.
Good provider-diverse review for hard diffs.
Use for high-stakes review and cross-repo context.

testing_qa.agile_context: Test strategy, unit/integration/e2e test generation, acceptance checks, QA scenarios, and regression verification.

recommended_models.why: Strong for coding tests and verification logic.
Good for iterative test generation and tool loops.
Useful private/offline high-reasoning test work.
Fast for simple unit tests and expected-output checks.
Good default for writing focused tests.

security_review.agile_context: Threat modeling, secure design review, vulnerability triage, auth/data boundary checks, and abuse-case review.

recommended_models.why: Use for high-stakes security analysis.
Strong for cybersecurity-oriented review and deep reasoning.
Strong independent security reasoning.
Good for broad system and document context.
Use when code or findings must stay local/private.

release_deployment.agile_context: Release plan, deployment checklist, CI/CD notes, rollback plan, migration steps, and release notes.

recommended_models.why: Good for practical deployment and automation tasks.
Good for multi-step tool and deployment planning.
Good for careful runbooks and release notes.
Good default for checklists and release engineering.
Fast for simple release-note drafting.

workspace_setup.agile_context: Set up or detect isolated workspaces, worktrees, repo baseline state, local environment, and pre-implementation verification.

recommended_models.why: Fast for bounded workspace and repository setup checks.
Use when setup touches several project conventions.
Good for multi-step setup in agentic tool loops.
Cheap option for simple setup and verification commands.
Default when setup needs platform judgment or recovery.

monitoring_incident_ops.agile_context: Monitoring design, incident triage, log synthesis, postmortems, service reliability, and operational follow-up.

recommended_models.why: Strong at root-cause analysis and long-context reasoning.
Strong for incident synthesis and remediation plans.
Good for hard multi-step ops reasoning.
Good for large docs/log context and multimodal evidence.
Good for long-running operational investigation.

documentation_knowledge_transfer.agile_context: Developer docs, ADRs, changelogs, onboarding docs, handoff notes, and knowledge-base cleanup.

recommended_models.why: Strong for polished knowledge work.
Good professional writing with code awareness.
Fast for straightforward docs and summaries.
Good default for clear engineering documentation.
Fast for low-risk formatting, summaries, and changelogs.

retrospective_process_improvement.agile_context: Retrospective facilitation, process metrics, working-agreement updates, and improvement experiments.

recommended_models.why: Strong for cross-signal reasoning and improvement planning.
Good for synthesizing broad qualitative and quantitative inputs.
Good for practical knowledge-work outputs.
Good for long-horizon process and org reasoning.
Default for normal retrospective notes and action items.
